overall_summary: OpenAI supports the DOE and Commission initiative to speed large-load interconnection and proposes several pathways to expedite ready, high-value loads such as AI data centers. It backs national-interest designation and fast-tracking for loads above 250 MW, an optional curtailable-load pathway, net-impact studies for hybrid load-plus-generation facilities, readiness-based prioritization with reduced withdrawal penalties for vetted projects, and letting commercially-ready loads fund and self-build network upgrades.

quotes.text: OpenAI supports the DOE's and the Commission's initiative to promote speed, reliability and clarity in the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system.
quotes.concern: Endorses the large-load interconnection reform initiative overall.

quotes.text: OpenAI supports an approach that allows the DOE to identify and provide a "national-interest" designation for loads above 250 MW that have clear national interest or security,economicimperativeorcompetitiveness,orcritical-infrastructurevalue
quotes.concern: Backs a DOE national-interest designation for loads above 250 MW.

quotes.text: Transmission providers shouldthenberequiredtoexpeditetheinterconnectionofthesedesignatedprojects through a fast track process.
quotes.concern: Wants a required fast-track interconnection for designated projects.

quotes.text: OpenAIsupportsanoptionalcurtailable-loadpathwaywherefullorpartialcurtailment obligations of the large loads allow thetransmissionprovidertoexpeditetheinterconnection request.
quotes.concern: Supports an optional curtailable-load pathway to expedite interconnection.

quotes.text: Largeloadsthatagreetocurtaildemandwhencalledonbysystemoperatorsfor a predetermined number of hours should be given interconnection priority over inflexible loads.
quotes.concern: Curtailable loads should get interconnection priority over inflexible loads.

quotes.text: OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generationtobeevaluatedonanet-impactbasistocapturerealsystemeffectsandreducestudy burdensandduration.
quotes.concern: Wants net-impact studies for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation.

quotes.text: OpenAI supports standardized readiness criteria that classify large loads based on strict metrics for commercial readiness, end user alignment, site and engineering maturity, and near-term operational timelines.
quotes.concern: Supports standardized readiness criteria to prioritize ready projects.

quotes.text: Forlargeloadprojectsthatmeetthereadinesscriteriaandhavesecuredvalidenduser agreements but subsequently withdraw for reasonsbeyondtheircontrol,withdrawalpenalties shouldbereduced.
quotes.concern: Reduce withdrawal penalties for vetted projects that withdraw for reasons beyond their control.

quotes.text: Speculativeloadsthatwithdrawwithoutenduserofftakeagreementsshould remain subject to full withdrawal penalties.
quotes.concern: Speculative loads without offtake agreements should keep full withdrawal penalties.
Let commercially-ready loads fund and self-build network upgrades to speed interconnection.

bins.name: AI leadership drives reform
bins.description: OpenAI frames the rulemaking as essential to U.S. competitiveness in AI and supports the DOE and Commission initiative to promote speed, reliability, and clarity in large-load interconnection.

bins.name: Fast-track high-value loads
bins.description: OpenAI wants designated national-interest projects to receive a required fast-track interconnection process, and supports expedited treatment for loads that accept curtailment.

bins.name: 250 MW national-interest threshold
bins.description: OpenAI proposes that DOE designate loads above 250 MW with national-interest, security, economic, or critical-infrastructure value for expedited treatment.

bins.name: Curtailable load priority
bins.description: OpenAI supports an optional curtailable-load pathway and argues loads that agree to curtail on operator call should get interconnection priority over inflexible loads.

bins.name: Net-impact study for hybrids
bins.description: OpenAI supports a pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation to be evaluated on a net-impact basis to capture real system effects and cut study burden.

bins.name: On-site and behind-meter pairing
bins.description: OpenAI backs co-located load-plus-generation configurations, asking that on-site or behind-the-meter generation be credited through net-impact evaluation.

bins.name: Readiness-based study and prioritization
bins.description: OpenAI supports standardized readiness criteria using strict commercial-readiness metrics, and net-impact studies that reduce study duration, to direct scarce study resources to vetted projects.

bins.name: Tiered withdrawal penalties
bins.description: OpenAI wants reduced withdrawal penalties for ready projects with valid end-user agreements that withdraw for reasons beyond their control, while keeping full penalties for speculative loads without offtake agreements.

bins.name: Self-fund network upgrades
bins.description: OpenAI supports letting commercially-ready loads fund and voluntarily build required network upgrades, subject to appropriate protections, when doing so accelerates interconnection.

bins.name: Load-funded upgrade costs
bins.description: OpenAI proposes that ready large loads bear the cost of constructing required network upgrades themselves to speed their interconnection.
